Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Jesus Chavez And Raul Garcia
Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Sydney Baba, Esq., PHILLIPS, SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP (415) 278-9400 Attorney for Defendant
Marie Grace Jennifer Pasilaban Wong, Trustee of the Philip Macdonald Family Trust for
No Other Appearances.

COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON HER BEHALF.

Motion is not contested. Counsel for defendant submits to the Court's tentative ruling. No Agument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARIE GRACE PASILABAN WONG'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Jesus Chavez And Raul Garcia

This is a wrongful eviction and premises defects action initiated by plaintiffs Chavez and Garcia. Many months ago, defendant propounded written discovery to plaintiffs consisting of form and special interrogatories and a request for production of documents. For a host of reasons, plaintiffs' counsel sought extension after extension which defendant granted. Eventually, patience wearing thin, and the last extension having expired, defendant filed the subject motion to compel seeking responses to the three discovery requests. Plaintiffs have not responded to the motion.

Since plaintiffs have neither provided responses to the subject discovery nor responded to defendant's motion, that motion to compel is GRANTED, with plaintiffs ordered to provide full, complete and objection-free responses within 20 days of the filing date of the Court's order. In view of the problems expressed by plaintiffs' counsel, affecting both counsel and clients, plaintiffs must decide whether to continue this litigation. In the meantime, discovery responses are due and this litigation cannot be held in abeyance any further. Plaintiffs cannot use the court system as a storage locker for claims. In the Court's discretion, although sanctions would ordinarily be appropriate here, on the facts of this case defendant's request for sanctions will be denied.

Counsel for defendant is directed to prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing and forward it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
